tldr: Adyen and Square are both PSPs but Square is SMB-focused with strong omnichannel POS, online checkout, and invoicing. The migration is a PCI-compliant vault transfer plus a rewrite against Square's APIs, and is best suited to teams whose payment volume and feature needs fit Square's SMB profile.
why_switch: Simpler self-serve onboarding for SMBs without enterprise sales cycles
Public, transaction-rate pricing that is easier to model than negotiated interchange++
Strong in-store POS hardware and reader ecosystem if omnichannel matters
Integrated invoicing and online checkout in one platform
Tighter fit for SMB operational needs — verify current plans and fees on the target's official pricing page
what_to_export: Tokenized card vault (handled via PCI-compliant transfer, not client export)
Customer payment methods and recurring agreements
Webhook endpoints and HMAC signing keys
Merchant account and store / location hierarchy
Catalog or product references tied to payments
export_method: Coordinate a PCI-DSS-compliant vault transfer between Adyen and Square via the standard PSP-to-PSP / network-token process; this is contractual and cannot be done as a client-side data export. In parallel, export shopper references, recurring agreements, refund and dispute history, and settlement reports from Adyen using its reporting tools. Save webhook configurations and signing keys. Capture the Adyen merchant account and store hierarchy, since Square models multi-location setups differently (locations within a Square account). Keep finance reports as your audit trail through cutover.
import_method: Onboard a Square account, complete KYC, and set up locations matching your Adyen store structure. Initiate the card vault transfer with both PSPs and your compliance team. Re-integrate against Square's Payments API (Web Payments SDK on the client, server SDKs for charges and refunds), including 3DS / SCA where needed. Re-implement webhook handlers against Square's event format and signature verification. If you take in-person payments, integrate readers and the Terminal API. Verify exact endpoints, supported regions, and current behavior in the Square official docs. Test in sandbox, run a low-traffic canary, then ramp. Keep Adyen active for in-flight disputes and refunds against pre-migration charges.

concept_mapping.note: Consult target's official pricing page for current per-transaction rates and any plan fees

effort: 2-4 months including KYC, vault transfer, and 3DS testing
gotchas: Square has narrower country coverage than Adyen; verify availability per market in current docs
Card vault transfer is contractual and PCI-controlled; cannot be client-driven
Per-country local payment methods may not match Adyen's coverage — audit your top markets
3DS / SCA flows must be re-implemented and tested per region
Refunds and disputes on pre-migration charges still flow through Adyen until they age out
Pricing and plan fees differ between Adyen and Square — rerun unit economics against the target's official pricing page rather than assumed numbers

email.subject: Migrating PSP from Adyen to Square — kickoff plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We are evaluating a move from Adyen to Square. The end-to-end project runs longer than two weeks because of KYC and the card-vault transfer, but here is the 14-day kickoff plan.

Days 1-3: confirm Square covers our markets and SMB feature needs, complete onboarding and KYC, and validate current rates on Square's official pricing page.
Days 4-7: kick off PCI-compliant vault transfer with both PSPs and our compliance team.
Days 8-11: rebuild integration on Web Payments SDK and server SDK in sandbox, including 3DS and webhooks.
Days 12-14: run sandbox QA across representative scenarios and finalize the canary plan.

Full production cutover will follow once vault transfer and 3DS testing are complete.

Thanks,
Your customer
